scale.method: blind review — scorer re-read every story fresh with no memory of prior scores

dimensions.L_literacy: Grammar naturalness, idiomatic phrasing, lexical fit
dimensions.N_narrative: Clear scene, beginning/middle/end, no abrupt jumps
dimensions.E_engagement: Voice, emotional pull, sensory detail, hook
dimensions.C_cohesion: Every sentence belongs to the story (penalises unrelated weaves/orphans)

rewritten_after_blind_review.method: Trim or integrate orphan tails into scene-resident sentences. No deletions of debut words/grammar. Cascade fixes (G014/G029/G032/G048 grammar accidents and W00120/W00083 vocab debut shifts) caught and corrected during the pass.

key_findings: Cohesion (C) is the library's weakest dimension. The pattern: a strong main scene followed by 1-6 trailing 'reuse weave' sentences that don't belong.
The very last sentence of many stories is an orphan vocab/grammar reinforcement — a uniform tax on otherwise excellent prose.
Stories 30 and 36 score perfect 25 — both have zero orphan weaves; every sentence serves the scene.
Story 10 (友達からの手紙) is the only ★★★ — 3 trailing fragments (犬は家にいます / 椅子に座ります) on a 6-sentence story is too high a ratio.
The bookstore-arc cluster (38-67) carries the library's most ambitious narrative — but also accumulates the longest tail-weaves because vocab inventory grows fast in the 50s/60s.
Late-library stories with 5-6 trailing fragments (story_56, 60, 63, 65, 66) score 21-23 instead of 24-25 — would be perfect if the orphan reuses were trimmed.
